experiment: F-SEC3: Is security self-assessment epistemically locked?
method: Classify all evidence sources for F-SEC1's 5 security layers as INTERNAL (swarm-designed), EXTERNAL (independent), or SYNTHETIC (adversarial input). Evidence sources: experiment JSONs, tools, guard scripts, lessons, tests.

layer_1_genesis_hash.description: FM-11 replay attack prevention via genesis_hash.py + guard 06
layer_1_genesis_hash.synthetic_detail: f_sec1_security_audit.py simulates tamper by injecting belief text

layer_3_belief_drift.description: FM-13 silent corruption detection via merge_back.py 10%/30% thresholds

layer_4_never_remove_atoms.description: FM-10 override attack prevention via guard 05-never-remove-atom.sh

layer_5_colony_depth_limit.description: Fork bomb prevention via MAX_COLONY_DEPTH=3 in swarm_colony.py

verdict_detail: Prediction confirmed with caveat: 3/5 layers are 100% INTERNAL. 2/5 have minimal SYNTHETIC evidence (1 source each). 0 EXTERNAL across all layers. Security self-assessment is epistemically locked.
prescriptions: Request external adversarial audit (human or third-party) for at least 1 security layer
Expand synthetic testing: adversarial input generation for trust-tier bypass and atom removal
Downgrade F-SEC1 confidence from RESOLVED to INTERNAL-ONLY until external validation exists
